note: vc_id lists are representative anchors and key members. CC must validate against complete is_relevant verse list per sub-group. The design documents (WA-M04-M04-A-vcg-design and WA-M04-M04-BCDEF-BOUNDARY-vcg-design) carry the full analytical rationale. VCG verse membership is term-based: all verses of a term route to that term's primary sub-group VCG per the mapping in WA-M04-subgroup-mapping-v1-20260517.json. Exceptions noted as dual-membership flags in design docs.

vcgs.description: Exultation in God: Joy Directed Toward YHWH as Object. Inner exultation, gladness, and rejoicing whose explicit object is YHWH himself — not what he has done but who he is. Includes 'rejoice IN the Lord' as a standing disposition (Phil 3:1; 4:4; 1Th 5:16; Hab 3:18), God named as 'my exceeding joy' (Psa 43:4), the soul rejoicing in God's salvation (Psa 35:9; Isa 61:10), hearts directed toward God's holy name (1Ch 16:10; Psa 13:5), and delight in the Lord as the orienting inner posture of the seeking person (Psa 37:4; a.nog).

Joy at God's Saving Acts: Gladness at What YHWH Has Done. Inner gladness evoked by specific divine acts of deliverance, victory, provision, or answered prayer. Joy is responsive to a particular event: Exo 18:9 (Jethro's gladness at hearing of deliverance), 1Sa 6:13 (rejoicing at returning ark), 1Sa 11:15 (exultation at victory), Isa 25:9 (willing glad response after waiting for God's salvation), Joe 2:21-23 (rejoicing at God's great deeds), Est 8-9 (joy at rescue from threatened destruction). Distinguished from VCG-01 by event-specific trigger rather than steady orientation toward God himself.

Divine Joy: God's Delight and Pleasure Directed Toward His People. God as the subject of delight and rejoicing directed toward persons or creation. Includes the Father's well-pleasure in the Son (eudokeo: Mat 3:17; 12:18; 17:5; Mar 1:11; Luk 3:22; 2Pe 1:17), God's sovereign pleasure and will (1Cor 1:21; Gal 1:16; Col 1:19; Luk 12:32), God's joy over his people (Zep 3:17; Jer 32:41; Isa 62:4-5; Deu 30:9), God's delight in steadfast love and righteousness (Jer 9:24; Hos 6:6; Mic 7:18; Eze 18:23; 33:11). Characteristic-in-operation per programme scope: divine joy/delight enacted toward his people belongs in M04.

NT Joy in Christ and the Spirit. Distinctively New Testament register: joy arising from believing in Christ, indwelt by the Spirit, and anchored in the gospel. Markers: Holy Spirit as agent or source of joy (Gal 5:22; Act 13:52; 1Th 1:6; Rom 14:17; Rom 15:13), Christ's own joy given to or indwelling disciples (Joh 15:11; 17:13), joy arising from receiving the word of Christ (Luk 2:10; Act 16:34; 1Pe 1:8). Also includes Mary's Spirit-located exultation (Luk 1:47), Jesus' Spirit-empowered rejoicing (Luk 10:21), and the euthumeo cheerfulness grounded in divine assurance (Act 27:22; 27:25; Jam 5:13).

Joy in Suffering: Paradoxical and Counter-intuitive Gladness. NT register of inner rejoicing that persists through or arises because of suffering, hardship, weakness, or dishonour endured for Christ. Key content: joy commanded in persecution (Mat 5:12; Luk 6:23; Jam 1:2), gladness at suffering dishonour for Christ's name (Act 5:41), joy at weakness and calamity for Christ (2Cor 12:10), rejoicing in sufferings for the church (Col 1:24), joy while poured out as sacrifice (Phil 2:17), the joy set before Jesus sustaining the cross (Heb 12:2), joyful acceptance of property loss (Heb 10:34). Also ba.lag (fragile cheerfulness under extreme adversity: Job 9:27; 10:20; Psa 39:13).

Worship-Joy: Gladness Expressed in Corporate Praise and Sacred Celebration. Joy that finds its natural expression in communal worship, song, and sacred ceremony. The Deuteronomy feast-commands (Deu 12:7, 12, 18; 16:11, 14; 26:11; 27:7; Lev 23:40), the Chronicles and Nehemiah worship scenes (2Ch 29:30; 30:21; Neh 12:27; 12:43), Psalm calls for rejoicing in song before God (Psa 32:11; 96:11; 97:1; 100:2; 149:2), and festive worship occasions (Neh 8:10, 12, 17; 1Ch 15:25; 16:31) define this register. Joy here cannot remain silent — it finds its voice in the gathered assembly through music, shout, and song.

Joy as Heart-Disposition: Gladness Located and Held in the Inner Person. Verses that foreground the heart or inner being as the explicit seat of gladness — joy as a constitutional inner disposition rather than a response to a specific event. Includes: Pro 17:22 (joyful heart as good medicine), Pro 15:13 (glad heart shining on the face), Pro 15:15 (cheerful heart as continual feast), Psa 4:7 (God puts joy in the heart), Psa 16:9 (heart gladness extending to whole being), Psa 19:8 (precepts rejoicing the heart), Psa 33:21 (heart named as where gladness resides), Ecc passages on heart-joy. Also the NT commands to constant, circumstance-independent joy as inner stance (1Th 5:16; 2Cor 13:11).

Inverted and Corrupt Joy: The Morally Dark Register. Verses where the joy vocabulary names inner gladness that is misdirected, morally perverted, or corrupt — joy over another's suffering, treacherous delight, hollow or idolatrous rejoicing, self-congratulatory gladness. Enemies rejoicing over the righteous (Psa 13:4; 30:1; 35:15, 19, 24; 38:16), treacherous delight (Mar 14:11; Luk 22:5), wicked's fleeting/hollow joy (Job 20:5; Isa 22:13), idolatrous rejoicing (Hos 9:1; 10:5), nations' corrupt joy over Israel's desolation (Eze 25:6; 35:14-15; Lam 2:17; 4:21). These evidence the joy register by its inversion.

Joy Arising from Human Faithfulness and Relational Bonds. Inner gladness that arises specifically from the faithfulness, love, spiritual growth, and presence of other persons. Paul's joy over the Thessalonians and Philippians so great no thanksgiving can match it (1Th 3:9; 2:19-20), joy fulfilled through community harmony (Phil 2:2), joy at another's faithfulness (2Jo 4; 3Jo 3; 3Jo 4), joy at another's spiritual renewal (2Cor 7:7; 7:13). OT parallels: parental joy at a wise child (Pro 10:1; 15:20; 23:24; 27:11), relational welcoming joy (Judg 9:19; 19:3). Distinct from VCG-04 in that the trigger is human relationship and faithfulness rather than divine action.

Delight as Volitional Inner Pleasure: The Will-Pleasure Register. Verses where cha.phets and che.phets function primarily as markers of inner volitional disposition — willing-pleasure, desire of the will, sovereign preference — rather than emotional gladness. Human willingness or unwillingness to perform duty (Deu 21:14; 25:7; 25:8; Rut 3:13), inner desire driving action (Gen 34:19; 1Ki 9:1; Est 6:6-11; Song 2:7; 3:5; 8:4 — love's own inner readiness), the pleasure underlying agreement or preference (1Ki 5:8-10; 10:13), Ecclesiastes' loss of will's capacity to enjoy (Ecc 12:1), divine sovereign purpose as pleasure (Isa 44:28; 46:10; 48:14; 53:10). The will-dimension of M04 distinct from emotional joy.

Shared Rejoicing: Joy Expressed and Received Together. Inner gladness that is constitutively shared — sought out, invited into, and completed in community. The shepherd calls neighbours to co-rejoice at the found sheep (Luk 15:6); the woman invites friends to share joy at the found coin (Luk 15:9); Elizabeth's mercy is shared by neighbours and relatives (Luk 1:58); the body members share gladness when one is honoured (1Cor 12:26); love co-rejoices with truth (1Cor 13:6). Ezr 6:16 anchors the OT side: communal emotional response to temple completion after exile.

Law-Delight: Affective Pleasure in God's Word, Statutes, and Testimonies. Concentrated inner register of pleasure taken in God's word, law, statutes, and testimonies as its named object. Psalm 119 series (sha.a: 119:16, 47, 70; sha.a.shu.im: 119:24, 77, 92, 143, 174) provide the core: affective pleasure that nourishes the soul, sustains through affliction, shapes decision-making, and distinguishes the inner-responsive person from the spiritually numb. Psalm 1:2 frames this as the defining inner disposition of the blessed person. Romans 7:22 (sunēdomai) explicitly locates this delight in the 'inner being.'

Relational and Parental Delight: God's Tenderness and the Carefree Delight of Peace. Register of tender, relational, affective pleasure: God's delight in Wisdom before creation (Pro 8:30), Wisdom's delight in humanity (Pro 8:31), God's treasured affection for Israel as pleasant planting (Isa 5:7), God's cherishing of Ephraim persisting through rebuke (Jer 31:20). Also the carefree, child-like delight picturing the age of peace: nursing child playing over a cobra's hole without fear (Isa 11:8), infant cradled in comfort (Isa 66:12). Distinct from VCG-01 in that the object is relationship and the register is tender cherishing rather than law-pleasure.

Joy of the Redeemed: Restoration Gladness and Promised Reversal of Sorrow. Inner gladness promised as the direct reversal of sorrow through God's redemptive acts. OT anchor: Isa 35:10; 51:3; 51:11 (everlasting joy crowning the ransomed), Isa 61:3 (oil of gladness over mourners), gi.lah (Isa 35:2; 65:18 — new creation rejoicing), Jer 31:13 (God pouring gladness to replace sorrow), Jer 33:9; 33:11 (gladness returning to the restored city), Zec 8:19 (fasts becoming feasts). Also personal salvation-joy: Psa 45:7, 51:8, 51:12, 105:43, 119:111, Isa 12:3; Est 8:16-17 (deliverance joy).

Joy Removed: Communal Gladness Stilled and Silenced by Judgment. Verses where sa.s.von describes the removal of communal joy by divine judgment — joy as what was and is now absent, its loss marking desolation and covenant consequence. God silences the voice of gladness in Judah's cities (Jer 7:34); mirth removed before the people's eyes (Jer 16:9); rejoicing banished by judgment (Jer 25:10); gladness dries up as crops fail (Joe 1:12); reckless festivity in the shadow of death (Isa 22:13). These define the M04-D characteristic by its devastating removal. Also Jer 15:16 (God's word as rejoicing to Jeremiah's heart — personal word-joy, assigned here as primary-D).

Divine Pleasantness: The Beauty and Favour of the LORD. Pleasantness attributed to God himself — his beauty, his character, or his covenantal favour — experienced by persons who seek and encounter him. Psa 27:4 (no.am names God's own being as the supreme object of longing), Psa 90:17 (Lord's pleasantness as outward-flowing grace), Psa 36:8 (God's river of delights lavishly supplied), Zec 11:7-10 (covenant pleasantness personified and then annulled). Pleasantness is here received from God, experienced in his presence, and characterizes divine favour.

Relational Pleasantness: The Felt Quality of Persons, Community, and Bonds. Pleasantness as the quality perceived and experienced in persons, relationships, and community. 2Sa 1:23 (Saul and Jonathan as lovely), 2Sa 1:26 (Jonathan's companionship deeply pleasant to David, surpassing even love of women — na.em in the soul), Psa 133:1 (brotherly unity pleasant), Song 1:16; 7:6 (beloved beautiful and pleasant). Also: pleasant inheritance (Psa 16:6), pleasantness of obedient life (Job 36:11), cherished plants as pleasant objects (Isa 17:10 — na.a.man), luxury/delight taken into exile (Jer 51:34 — ed.nah).

Pleasantness of Wisdom, Speech, and Instruction. Pleasantness characterizing wise, gracious, and true speech or instruction — the agreeableness of words that heal, nourish, and instruct. Wisdom's ways as pleasantness itself (Pro 3:17), gracious words as honey nourishing soul and body (Pro 16:24), gracious words pure before the Lord (Pro 15:26), wisdom's words pleasant within the person (Pro 22:18), knowledge pleasant to the soul (Pro 2:10), praise as pleasant and fitting (Psa 135:3; 147:1). Also: Pro 9:17 (deceptive pleasantness of forbidden bread), Pro 24:25 (delight as reward for moral courage), Psa 141:6 (psalmist's words heard as pleasant when vindicated).

Israel's Remembrance and Proclamation of God's Wondrous Works. Inner disposition of wonder at God's historical, redemptive, and creation wonders — held in memory, proclaimed publicly, and passed to the next generation as an act of faith and worship. The Psalm meditations on divine wonders (Psa 9:1; 26:7; 78:4; 105:2; 107 refrain-series; 111:4; 118:23; 136:4; 139:14; 145:5), the Exodus proclamation (Exo 15:11 — pe.le), the Chronicles calls to tell of God's wondrous works (1Ch 16:9, 12, 24). Wonder here is active-memory and wonder-as-proclamation-impulse — the astonishment that must be told.

Wonder at the Incomprehensible: Awe Before God's Surpassing Ways. Register where pa.la names God's acts or ways as exceeding human comprehension — wonder as the inner posture before the incomprehensible. Job's corpus: God's marvelous deeds beyond number (Job 5:9; 9:10), Job commanded to consider God's wondrous works (Job 37:14), Job recognizing he spoke of things too wonderful for him (Job 42:3 — definitive humility before incomprehensibility). God's counsel as wondrous (Isa 28:29; 29:14). Psa 131:1 (wonder consciously restrained). The 'is anything too wonderful for the LORD?' formula (Gen 18:14; Jer 32:17; 32:27). miph.la.ah at Job 37:16 (wondrous balance of clouds exceeding Job's knowledge).

Wonder as Extraordinary and Too-Difficult: The Legal, Relational, and Hardship Register. Verses where pa.la functions in its 'too-difficult / beyond ordinary capacity' register rather than evidencing inner-being wonder-response as such. Legal cases too complex for ordinary adjudication (Deu 17:8), commandment declared not too-hard (Deu 30:11 — accessible, not wonder-invoking), Jonathan's love as 'extraordinary' surpassing ordinary affection (2Sa 1:26), Amnon's frustrated incapacity as impossible barrier intensifying inner torment (2Sa 13:2), temple described as wonderful as an object-quality rather than inner experience (2Ch 2:9).

Wonder Inverted: Awe-Inspiring Judgment, Devastation, and Dark Wonders. Verses where pa.la and pe.le describe extraordinary occurrences producing awe-tinged dread rather than worshipful wonder: divine judgment as beyond-normal afflictions (Deu 28:59 — dread not joy), God hunting Job with wonders used as instruments of punishment (Job 10:16), fearful destruction of Dan 8:24, blasphemous astonishing speech of Dan 11:36, the appalling astonishment at Jerusalem's catastrophic fall (Lam 1:9 — pe.le applied to catastrophe), bewildering awe-inducing character of end-time events (Dan 12:6 — pe.le). Wonder here is inverted — stunned confrontation with incomprehensible ruin or evil.

Aggregating VCG for BOUNDARY terms — pending researcher disposition at Phase 12. Members span 15 terms: thanksgiving vocabulary (eucharistia 15 verses, eucharistos 1 verse; possible M21/M22 destination), troubled wonder (thambeō 3, thauma 2, thaumazō 5; possible M01 destination), blessedness (makarismos 3; possible M39 destination), pleasing-fitting register (arestos 4; possible M13/M30 destination), erotic-attractive delight (che.med 5; possible M28/M29), the 230-verse tov corpus (moral-quality, volitional-deference, and pleasantness sub-registers; Phase 12 split needed using Phase 3 cross-routing flag 4 map), priestly pleasing-aroma formula (ni.cho.ach 43 verses; possible M21 destination), predatory exultation (a.li.tsut 1 verse; possible M06), illicit/denied pleasure (a.las 2; possible M28/M29), dainty-luxury (a.nog-dainty 3; possible M46), empty corpus (te.mah 0 active verses), luxury-sensory delight (ta.a.nug 5; possible M29). No characteristic-bearing analysis performed at Phase 7.
vcgs.verses_note: All 322 is_relevant verses of the 15 BOUNDARY terms route to this VCG. vc_ids sourced from CC's sub-group routing apply output.
